message:
fix(text, textarea): handle CRLF line endings in cursor and text wrapping

The cursor module's strip_trailing_newline only removed '\n', leaving
'\r' behind for files with Windows-style CRLF endings. This caused
grapheme counting and cursor positioning to include the carriage return
as a visible character, leading to off-by-one cursor placement at line
ends.

The textarea widget had the same issue across all 14 call sites where
line text is fetched from the rope and stripped before wrap measurement.
Without stripping '\r', wrap width calculations were inflated by one
character per line, causing misaligned rendering and incorrect scroll
anchor computation on CRLF content.

Both now strip '\r' after '\n' to correctly handle CR+LF, standalone LF,
and standalone CR line endings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
